mission.title: Install and Configure ExternalDNS
mission.description: ExternalDNS is an operator that synchronizes Kubernetes Services and Ingresses with DNS providers, allowing for dynamic DNS record management. It is particularly useful in environments where services are frequently created and destroyed, ensuring that DNS records are always up-to-date. Key trade-offs include the need for proper permissions and potential delays in DNS propagation.

steps.title: Add the ExternalDNS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ExternalDNS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add external-dns https://kubernetes-sigs.github.io/external-dns/

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install ExternalDNS with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs ExternalDNS in the 'kube-system' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions set for your DNS provider.

steps.title: Create an ExternalDNS Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) that defines the desired DNS records and configurations.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
spec:
  provider: aws
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  policy: upsert-only
  service: true
  ingress: true
EOF

```

> Replace 'aws' and 'example.com' with your DNS provider and domain.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
spec:
  provider: aws
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  policy: upsert-only
  service: true
  ingress: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify ExternalDNS is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ExternalDNS deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=external-dns

```

> You should see the ExternalDNS pod in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ExternalDNS
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ExternalDNS installation and may lead to orphaned DNS records in your provider. Ensure to clean up any DNS records manually if necessary.

```bash

helm uninstall external-dns --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
EOF

```

> This will remove the ExternalDNS deployment.
> This will delete the Custom Resource.
commands:
kubectl delete -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
EOF

upgrade.title: Upgrade ExternalDNS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your current configuration. This step will upgrade ExternalDNS to the latest version while preserving your settings.

```bash

helm get values external-dns --namespace kube-system > external-dns-backup.yaml

helm upgrade external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=external-dns

```

> Backup current values.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the new version is running.
commands: helm get values external-dns --namespace kube-system > external-dns-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: Failed to create DNS record
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient permissions for the DNS provider.

**Fix:**
Check and update the IAM roles or permissions for the DNS provider.

troubleshooting.title: ExternalDNS pod is CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Misconfiguration in the ExternalDNS Custom Resource.

**Fix:**
Review the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' and correct the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: DNS records not updating
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** ExternalDNS is not able to communicate with the DNS provider.

**Fix:**
Verify the provider settings and network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: ExternalDNS not detecting Services or Ingresses
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Labels or annotations required for ExternalDNS are missing.

**Fix:**
Ensure Services and Ingresses have the correct annotations for ExternalDNS.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Azure DNS and improved handling of multiple domain filters.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the ExternalDNS pod running in the kube-system namespace with the command 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=external-dns' returning a status of 'Running'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
